modules.CausalSmith.Experimentation.EXP_Bipartit:
### Smooth envelope extension

The original envelope is singular at the coordinate hyperplanes `p_k = 0` and `p_k = 1`. The cutoff
reciprocal `recipC` (agrees with `x⁻¹` on the feasible box, globally `C¹`) was promoted to
`Causalean.Mathlib.Analysis.SmoothReciprocal` (`recipC`, `recipC_eq_inv`, `recipC_contDiff`, opened
via `Causalean.Mathlib`).
### `C²` regularity of the floored envelope extension

## Environment S2 — the bipartite interference / potential-outcome world

`BipartiteExperiment I O` bundles the known bipartite graph `G_n` (as the
intervention neighborhoods `N i = N_i(G_n)`) and the fixed potential-outcome
schedule `Yfun i` (as a function of the treatment vector, later restricted to
`N i` by `ass:bipartite-interference`). The intervention index type `I` realizes
`I_n` (and `m_n = card I`) and the outcome index type `O` realizes `O_n`
(and `n = card O`).

entries.statement: (I : Type u_1) → (O : Type u_2) → [Fintype I] → [Fintype O] → [DecidableEq I] → Type (max u_1 u_2)
entries.source:
/-- A bipartite experiment: the known bipartite graph (via outcome-side
neighborhoods) plus the fixed potential-outcome schedule. -/
structure BipartiteExperiment (I O : Type*) [Fintype I] [Fintype O] [DecidableEq I] where
  /-- Intervention neighborhood of each outcome unit. -/
  N : O → Finset I                 -- @realizes N_i(G_n)(N i = intervention neighborhood {k : (k,i) ∈ G_n})
  /-- Fixed potential-outcome schedule as a function of the treatment vector. -/
  Yfun : O → (I → Bool) → ℝ        -- @realizes Y_i(z_{N_i})(carrier; range ℝ); @realizes Z(assignment-vector argument I→Bool = {0,1}^{m_n}); @realizes Z_k(per-unit coordinate z k : Bool = {0,1})
entries.name: CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment
entries.doc:
A bipartite experiment: the known bipartite graph (via outcome-side
neighborhoods) plus the fixed potential-outcome schedule.

entries.statement: {I : Type u_1} → {O : Type u_2} → [inst : Fintype I] → [inst_1 : Fintype O] → [inst_2 : DecidableEq I] → CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment I O → I → Finset O
entries.source:
/-- Outcome neighborhood of an intervention unit `M_k(G_n) = {i : k ∈ N_i}`. -/
def M (k : I) : Finset O := Finset.univ.filter (fun i => k ∈ E.N i)   -- @realizes M_k(G_n)(outcome neighborhood)
entries.name: CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment.M
entries.doc: Outcome neighborhood of an intervention unit `M_k(G_n) = {i : k ∈ N_i}`.

entries.statement: {I : Type u_1} → {O : Type u_2} → [inst : Fintype I] → [inst_1 : Fintype O] → [inst_2 : DecidableEq I] → CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment I O → O → ℝ
entries.source:
/-- All-control potential outcome `Y_i^0 = Y_i(0_{N_i})`. -/
noncomputable def Y0 (i : O) : ℝ := E.Yfun i (fun _ => false)  -- @realizes Y_i^0(Yfun at all-control)
entries.name: CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment.Y0
entries.doc: All-control potential outcome `Y_i^0 = Y_i(0_{N_i})`.

entries.statement: {I : Type u_1} → {O : Type u_2} → [inst : Fintype I] → [inst_1 : Fintype O] → [inst_2 : DecidableEq I] → CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment I O → O → ℝ
entries.source:
/-- All-treated potential outcome `Y_i^1 = Y_i(1_{N_i})`. -/
noncomputable def Y1 (i : O) : ℝ := E.Yfun i (fun _ => true)   -- @realizes Y_i^1(Yfun at all-treated)

entries.statement: ∀ {I : Type u_1} {O : Type u_2} [inst : Fintype I] [inst_1 : Fintype O] [inst_2 : DecidableEq I] (E : CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment I O) (ε B : ℝ), 0 < ε → ε < 1 / 2 → ↑(Fintype.card I) * ε ≤ B → B ≤ ↑(Fintype.card I) * (1 - ε) → ∀ (d : I → ℝ), BddAbove (Set.range fun q => E.envCurv ε d ↑q)
entries.source:
/-- The curvature values over the feasible set are bounded above: `envCurv` is continuous and
`feasibleSet` is compact. This is the `BddAbove` side condition of `le_ciSup`, and the fact whose
absence stalled the original gate. -/
lemma bddAbove_envCurv_range (ε B : ℝ) (hε : 0 < ε) (hε2 : ε < 1 / 2)
    (hBlo : (Fintype.card I : ℝ) * ε ≤ B) (hBhi : B ≤ (Fintype.card I : ℝ) * (1 - ε))
    (d : I → ℝ) :
    BddAbove (Set.range fun q : feasibleSet (I := I) ε B => E.envCurv ε d (q : I → ℝ)) := by
  have hcpt : IsCompact (feasibleSet (I := I) ε B) :=
    (convex_design E ε B hε hε2 hBlo hBhi).2.1
  have hrange :
      Set.range (fun q : feasibleSet (I := I) ε B => E.envCurv ε d (q : I → ℝ)) =
        E.envCurv ε d '' feasibleSet (I := I) ε B := by
    ext x
    constructor
    · rintro ⟨q, rfl⟩
      exact ⟨q, q.property, rfl⟩
    · rintro ⟨q, hq, rfl⟩
      exact ⟨⟨q, hq⟩, rfl⟩
  rw [hrange]
  exact (hcpt.image (E.envCurv_continuous ε hε d)).bddAbove
entries.name: CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment.bddAbove_envCurv_range
entries.doc:
The curvature values over the feasible set are bounded above: `envCurv` is continuous and
`feasibleSet` is compact. This is the `BddAbove` side condition of `le_ciSup`, and the fact whose
absence stalled the original gate.

entries.statement: ∀ {I : Type u_1} {O : Type u_2} [inst : Fintype I] [inst_1 : Fintype O] [inst_2 : DecidableEq I] (E : CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment I O) (ε : ℝ), 0 < ε → ∀ {q d : I → ℝ} {S : Set ℝ}, (∀ s ∈ S, ∀ (k : I), ε / 2 ≤ q k + s * d k ∧ ε / 2 ≤ 1 - (q k + s * d k)) → ContDiffOn ℝ 2 (fun s => (E.varEnvelope fun k => q k + s * d k) / 4) S
entries.source:
/-- The envelope line is `C²` on any parameter set that keeps the line inside the saturated box. -/
lemma contDiffOn_envelope_line (ε : ℝ) (hε : 0 < ε) {q d : I → ℝ} {S : Set ℝ}
    (hbox : ∀ s ∈ S, ∀ k, ε / 2 ≤ q k + s * d k ∧ ε / 2 ≤ 1 - (q k + s * d k)) :
    ContDiffOn ℝ 2 (fun s : ℝ => E.varEnvelope (fun k => q k + s * d k) / 4) S := by
  have hext : ContDiff ℝ 2
      (fun s : ℝ => E.varEnvelopeExt ε (fun k => q k + s * d k) / 4) := by
    apply (E.envelopeQuarterExt_contDiff_two ε hε).comp
    apply contDiff_pi.mpr
    intro k
    fun_prop
  apply hext.contDiffOn.congr
  intro s hs
  exact congrArg (fun x : ℝ => x / 4)
    (E.varEnvelopeExt_eq_varEnvelope_of_box hε (fun k => (hbox s hs k).1)
      (fun k => (hbox s hs k).2)).symm
entries.name: CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment.contDiffOn_envelope_line
entries.doc: The envelope line is `C²` on any parameter set that keeps the line inside the saturated box.

entries.statement: {I : Type u_1} → {O : Type u_2} → [inst : Fintype I] → [inst_1 : Fintype O] → [inst_2 : DecidableEq I] → CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment I O → O → ℕ
entries.source:
/-- Outcome-side degree `d_i = |N_i(G_n)|`. -/
def deg (i : O) : ℕ := (E.N i).card   -- @realizes d_i(card N_i)
entries.name: CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment.deg
entries.doc: Outcome-side degree `d_i = |N_i(G_n)|`.

entries.statement: ∀ {I : Type u_1} {O : Type u_2} [inst : Fintype I] [inst_1 : Fintype O] [inst_2 : DecidableEq I] (E : CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment I O) (ε B : ℝ), 0 < ε → ∀ {q d : I → ℝ}, (∀ (k : I), |d k| ≤ 1) → ∀ (t : ℝ), (fun k => q k + t * d k) ∈ CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.feasibleSet ε B → deriv (deriv fun s => (E.varEnvelope fun k => q k + s * d k) / 4) t = E.envCurv ε d fun k => q k + t * d k
entries.source:
/-- **Second derivative of the envelope line at an arbitrary parameter `t`** equals the directional
curvature at the SHIFTED base point `q + t·d`. This is the form the `EnvelopeLineC2Data` curvature
conjunct needs, since it quantifies over `t ∈ Icc 0 T`. -/
lemma deriv_deriv_envelope_line_eq_envCurv (ε B : ℝ) (hε : 0 < ε) {q d : I → ℝ}
    (hd : ∀ k, |d k| ≤ 1) (t : ℝ)
    (hqt : (fun k => q k + t * d k) ∈ feasibleSet (I := I) ε B) :
    deriv (deriv fun s : ℝ => E.varEnvelope (fun k => q k + s * d k) / 4) t =
      E.envCurv ε d (fun k => q k + t * d k) := by
  let F : ℝ → ℝ := fun s => E.varEnvelope (fun k => q k + s * d k) / 4
  have hshift :
      (fun u : ℝ => F (t + u)) =
        (fun u : ℝ => E.varEnvelope (fun k => (q k + t * d k) + u * d k) / 4) := by
    funext u
    apply congrArg (fun p : I → ℝ => E.varEnvelope p / 4)
    funext k
    ring
  calc
    deriv (deriv F) t = deriv (fun u : ℝ => deriv F (t + u)) 0 := by
      symm
      simpa using (deriv_comp_const_add (deriv F) t 0)
    _ = deriv (deriv fun u : ℝ => F (t + u)) 0 := by
      congr 1
      funext u
      exact (deriv_comp_const_add F t u).symm
    _ = deriv (deriv fun u : ℝ =>
        E.varEnvelope (fun k => (q k + t * d k) + u * d k) / 4) 0 := by
      rw [hshift]
    _ = E.envCurv ε d (fun k => q k + t * d k) :=
      E.deriv_deriv_envelope_line_zero ε B hε hqt hd
entries.name: CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment.deriv_deriv_envelope_line_eq_envCurv
entries.doc:
**Second derivative of the envelope line at an arbitrary parameter `t`** equals the directional
curvature at the SHIFTED base point `q + t·d`. This is the form the `EnvelopeLineC2Data` curvature
conjunct needs, since it quantifies over `t ∈ Icc 0 T`.

entries.statement: ∀ {I : Type u_1} {O : Type u_2} [inst : Fintype I] [inst_1 : Fintype O] [inst_2 : DecidableEq I] (E : CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment I O) (ε B : ℝ), 0 < ε → ∀ {q : I → ℝ}, q ∈ CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.feasibleSet ε B → ∀ {d : I → ℝ}, (∀ (k : I), |d k| ≤ 1) → deriv (deriv fun s => (E.varEnvelope fun k => q k + s * d k) / 4) 0 = E.envCurv ε d q
entries.source:
/-- **Second derivative of the envelope line at `0`** equals the directional curvature at the base
point. -/
lemma deriv_deriv_envelope_line_zero (ε B : ℝ) (hε : 0 < ε) {q : I → ℝ}
    (hq : q ∈ feasibleSet (I := I) ε B) {d : I → ℝ} (hd : ∀ k, |d k| ≤ 1) :
    deriv (deriv fun s : ℝ => E.varEnvelope (fun k => q k + s * d k) / 4) 0 =
      E.envCurv ε d q := by
  let f : (I → ℝ) → ℝ := fun p => E.varEnvelopeExt ε p / 4
  have hline :
      (fun s : ℝ => E.varEnvelopeExt ε (fun k => q k + s * d k) / 4) =
        (fun s : ℝ => f (q + s • d)) := by
    funext s
    apply congrArg (fun p : I → ℝ => E.varEnvelopeExt ε p / 4)
    funext k
    simp [smul_eq_mul]
  calc
    deriv (deriv fun s : ℝ => E.varEnvelope (fun k => q k + s * d k) / 4) 0 =
        deriv (deriv fun s : ℝ => E.varEnvelopeExt ε (fun k => q k + s * d k) / 4) 0 :=
      (E.envelope_line_eventuallyEq ε B hε hq hd).deriv.deriv_eq
    _ = deriv (deriv fun s : ℝ => f (q + s • d)) 0 := by rw [hline]
    _ = secondDirDeriv f d q := by
      simpa [f] using deriv_deriv_line (E.envelopeQuarterExt_contDiff_two ε hε) q d 0
    _ = E.envCurv ε d q := rfl
entries.name: CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment.deriv_deriv_envelope_line_zero
entries.doc:
**Second derivative of the envelope line at `0`** equals the directional curvature at the base
point.

entries.statement: ∀ {I : Type u_1} {O : Type u_2} [inst : Fintype I] [inst_1 : Fintype O] [inst_2 : DecidableEq I] (E : CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment I O) (ε B : ℝ), 0 < ε → ∀ {q : I → ℝ}, q ∈ CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.feasibleSet ε B → ∀ {d : I → ℝ}, (∀ (k : I), |d k| ≤ 1) → deriv (fun s => (E.varEnvelope fun k => q k + s * d k) / 4) 0 = ∑ k, d k * E.envelopeGrad q k
entries.source:
/-- **First derivative of the envelope line at `0`.** -/
lemma deriv_envelope_line_zero (ε B : ℝ) (hε : 0 < ε) {q : I → ℝ}
    (hq : q ∈ feasibleSet (I := I) ε B) {d : I → ℝ} (hd : ∀ k, |d k| ≤ 1) :
    deriv (fun s : ℝ => E.varEnvelope (fun k => q k + s * d k) / 4) 0 =
      ∑ k, d k * E.envelopeGrad q k := by
  let f : (I → ℝ) → ℝ := fun p => E.varEnvelopeExt ε p / 4
  have hline :
      (fun s : ℝ => E.varEnvelopeExt ε (fun k => q k + s * d k) / 4) =
        (fun s : ℝ => f (q + s • d)) := by
    funext s
    apply congrArg (fun p : I → ℝ => E.varEnvelopeExt ε p / 4)
    funext k
    simp [smul_eq_mul]
  calc
    deriv (fun s : ℝ => E.varEnvelope (fun k => q k + s * d k) / 4) 0 =
        deriv (fun s : ℝ => E.varEnvelopeExt ε (fun k => q k + s * d k) / 4) 0 :=
      (E.envelope_line_eventuallyEq ε B hε hq hd).deriv_eq
    _ = deriv (fun s : ℝ => f (q + s • d)) 0 := by rw [hline]
    _ = fderiv ℝ f q d := by
      rw [deriv_line]
      · simp
      · simpa [f] using (E.envelopeQuarterExt_contDiff_two ε hε).differentiable two_ne_zero
    _ = ∑ k, d k * E.envelopeGrad q k :=
      E.fderiv_envelopeQuarterExt_apply ε B hε hq d
entries.name: CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment.deriv_envelope_line_zero
entries.doc: **First derivative of the envelope line at `0`.**

entries.statement: ∀ {I : Type u_1} {O : Type u_2} [inst : Fintype I] [inst_1 : Fintype O] [inst_2 : DecidableEq I] (E : CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment I O) (ε B : ℝ), 0 < ε → ∀ {q : I → ℝ}, q ∈ CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.feasibleSet ε B → ∀ {d : I → ℝ}, (∀ (k : I), |d k| ≤ 1) → DifferentiableAt ℝ (deriv fun s => (E.varEnvelope fun k => q k + s * d k) / 4) 0
entries.source:
/-- The DERIVATIVE of the envelope line is itself differentiable at `0` (the line is `C²` there).
This is the `hdd` hypothesis of `convexOn_deriv2_nonneg`. -/
lemma differentiableAt_deriv_envelope_line_zero (ε B : ℝ) (hε : 0 < ε) {q : I → ℝ}
    (hq : q ∈ feasibleSet (I := I) ε B) {d : I → ℝ} (hd : ∀ k, |d k| ≤ 1) :
    DifferentiableAt ℝ (deriv fun s : ℝ => E.varEnvelope (fun k => q k + s * d k) / 4) 0 := by
  apply (E.envelope_line_eventuallyEq ε B hε hq hd).deriv.differentiableAt_iff.mpr
  have hext : ContDiff ℝ 2
      (fun s : ℝ => E.varEnvelopeExt ε (fun k => q k + s * d k) / 4) := by
    apply (E.envelopeQuarterExt_contDiff_two ε hε).comp
    apply contDiff_pi.mpr
    intro k
    fun_prop
  have hder : ContDiff ℝ 1
      (deriv fun s : ℝ => E.varEnvelopeExt ε (fun k => q k + s * d k) / 4) := by
    exact (contDiff_succ_iff_deriv.mp hext).2.2
  exact (hder.differentiable one_ne_zero).differentiableAt
entries.name: CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment.differentiableAt_deriv_envelope_line_zero
entries.doc:
The DERIVATIVE of the envelope line is itself differentiable at `0` (the line is `C²` there).
This is the `hdd` hypothesis of `convexOn_deriv2_nonneg`.

entries.statement: ∀ {I : Type u_1} {O : Type u_2} [inst : Fintype I] [inst_1 : Fintype O] [inst_2 : DecidableEq I] (E : CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment I O) (ε B : ℝ), 0 < ε → ∀ {q d : I → ℝ}, (∀ (k : I), |d k| ≤ 1) → ∀ (t : ℝ), (fun k => q k + t * d k) ∈ CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.feasibleSet ε B → DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun s => (E.varEnvelope fun k => q k + s * d k) / 4) t
entries.source:
/-- The envelope line is differentiable at ANY parameter whose base point is feasible. Needed to
feed `ConvexOn.monotoneOn_deriv` on the whole segment, not just at its midpoint. -/
lemma differentiableAt_envelope_line (ε B : ℝ) (hε : 0 < ε) {q d : I → ℝ}
    (hd : ∀ k, |d k| ≤ 1) (t : ℝ)
    (hqt : (fun k => q k + t * d k) ∈ feasibleSet (I := I) ε B) :
    DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun s : ℝ => E.varEnvelope (fun k => q k + s * d k) / 4) t := by
  have hshift : DifferentiableAt ℝ
      (fun u : ℝ => E.varEnvelope (fun k => (q k + t * d k) + u * d k) / 4) 0 :=
    E.differentiableAt_envelope_line_zero ε B hε hqt hd
  have heq :
      (fun u : ℝ => E.varEnvelope (fun k => q k + (t + u) * d k) / 4) =
        (fun u : ℝ => E.varEnvelope (fun k => (q k + t * d k) + u * d k) / 4) := by
    funext u
    congr 2
    funext k
    ring
  apply (differentiableAt_iff_comp_const_add (f :=
    fun s : ℝ => E.varEnvelope (fun k => q k + s * d k) / 4) (a := t) (b := t)).mpr
  rw [heq]
  simpa using hshift
entries.name: CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment.differentiableAt_envelope_line
entries.doc:
The envelope line is differentiable at ANY parameter whose base point is feasible. Needed to
feed `ConvexOn.monotoneOn_deriv` on the whole segment, not just at its midpoint.

entries.statement: ∀ {I : Type u_1} {O : Type u_2} [inst : Fintype I] [inst_1 : Fintype O] [inst_2 : DecidableEq I] (E : CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment I O) (ε B : ℝ), 0 < ε → ∀ {q : I → ℝ}, q ∈ CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.feasibleSet ε B → ∀ {d : I → ℝ}, (∀ (k : I), |d k| ≤ 1) → DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun s => (E.varEnvelope fun k => q k + s * d k) / 4) 0
entries.source:
/-- The envelope line through a feasible point is differentiable at `0`. -/
lemma differentiableAt_envelope_line_zero (ε B : ℝ) (hε : 0 < ε) {q : I → ℝ}
    (hq : q ∈ feasibleSet (I := I) ε B) {d : I → ℝ} (hd : ∀ k, |d k| ≤ 1) :
    DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun s : ℝ => E.varEnvelope (fun k => q k + s * d k) / 4) 0 := by
  apply (E.envelope_line_eventuallyEq ε B hε hq hd).differentiableAt_iff.mpr
  have hext : ContDiff ℝ 2
      (fun s : ℝ => E.varEnvelopeExt ε (fun k => q k + s * d k) / 4) := by
    apply (E.envelopeQuarterExt_contDiff_two ε hε).comp
    apply contDiff_pi.mpr
    intro k
    fun_prop
  exact (hext.differentiable two_ne_zero).differentiableAt
entries.name: CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment.differentiableAt_envelope_line_zero
entries.doc: The envelope line through a feasible point is differentiable at `0`.

entries.statement: ∀ {I : Type u_1} {O : Type u_2} [inst : Fintype I] [inst_1 : Fintype O] [inst_2 : DecidableEq I] (E : CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment I O) (ε B : ℝ), 0 < ε → ∀ {d : I → ℝ}, (∀ (k : I), |d k| ≤ 1) → CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.dirModulus E ε B d = ⨆ q, E.envCurv ε d ↑q
entries.source:
/-- `dirModulus` is the supremum of `envCurv` over the feasible set: its defining body, stated with
the singular `V_env`, agrees with `envCurv` at every feasible base point. -/
lemma dirModulus_eq_ciSup_envCurv (ε B : ℝ) (hε : 0 < ε) {d : I → ℝ} (hd : ∀ k, |d k| ≤ 1) :
    dirModulus E ε B d = ⨆ q : feasibleSet (I := I) ε B, E.envCurv ε d (q : I → ℝ) := by
  unfold dirModulus
  congr 1
  funext q
  exact E.deriv_deriv_envelope_line_zero ε B hε q.property hd
entries.name: CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment.dirModulus_eq_ciSup_envCurv
entries.doc:
`dirModulus` is the supremum of `envCurv` over the feasible set: its defining body, stated with
the singular `V_env`, agrees with `envCurv` at every feasible base point.

entries.statement: {I : Type u_1} → {O : Type u_2} → [inst : Fintype I] → [inst_1 : Fintype O] → [inst_2 : DecidableEq I] → CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment I O → ℝ → (I → ℝ) → (I → ℝ) → ℝ
entries.source:
/-- Directional curvature of the normalized envelope at base point `q` along `d`, i.e.
`Hess (V_env/4) q (d, d)`, computed on the globally-`C²` extension. -/
noncomputable def envCurv (E : BipartiteExperiment I O) (ε : ℝ) (d q : I → ℝ) : ℝ :=
  secondDirDeriv (fun p : I → ℝ => E.varEnvelopeExt ε p / 4) d q
entries.name: CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment.envCurv
entries.doc:
Directional curvature of the normalized envelope at base point `q` along `d`, i.e.
`Hess (V_env/4) q (d, d)`, computed on the globally-`C²` extension.

entries.statement: ∀ {I : Type u_1} {O : Type u_2} [inst : Fintype I] [inst_1 : Fintype O] [inst_2 : DecidableEq I] (E : CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment I O) (ε : ℝ), 0 < ε → ∀ (d : I → ℝ), Continuous (E.envCurv ε d)
entries.source:
/-- The curvature modulus is continuous in the base point. Combined with compactness of
`feasibleSet` this is what makes `dirModulus` a genuine (bounded) supremum. -/
lemma envCurv_continuous (ε : ℝ) (hε : 0 < ε) (d : I → ℝ) :
    Continuous (E.envCurv ε d) := by
  exact continuous_secondDirDeriv (E.envelopeQuarterExt_contDiff_two ε hε) d
entries.name: CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment.envCurv_continuous
entries.doc:
The curvature modulus is continuous in the base point. Combined with compactness of
`feasibleSet` this is what makes `dirModulus` a genuine (bounded) supremum.

entries.statement: ∀ {I : Type u_1} {O : Type u_2} [inst : Fintype I] [inst_1 : Fintype O] [inst_2 : DecidableEq I] (E : CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment I O) (ε B : ℝ), 0 < ε → ε < 1 / 2 → ↑(Fintype.card I) * ε ≤ B → B ≤ ↑(Fintype.card I) * (1 - ε) → ∀ {d : I → ℝ}, (∀ (k : I), |d k| ≤ 1) → ∀ {q : I → ℝ}, q ∈ CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.feasibleSet ε B → E.envCurv ε d q ≤ CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.dirModulus E ε B d
entries.source:
/-- Every feasible base point's directional curvature is dominated by `dirModulus`. -/
lemma envCurv_le_dirModulus (ε B : ℝ) (hε : 0 < ε) (hε2 : ε < 1 / 2)
    (hBlo : (Fintype.card I : ℝ) * ε ≤ B) (hBhi : B ≤ (Fintype.card I : ℝ) * (1 - ε))
    {d : I → ℝ} (hd : ∀ k, |d k| ≤ 1) {q : I → ℝ} (hq : q ∈ feasibleSet (I := I) ε B) :
    E.envCurv ε d q ≤ dirModulus E ε B d := by
  rw [E.dirModulus_eq_ciSup_envCurv ε B hε hd]
  exact le_ciSup (E.bddAbove_envCurv_range ε B hε hε2 hBlo hBhi d) ⟨q, hq⟩
entries.name: CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment.envCurv_le_dirModulus
entries.doc: Every feasible base point's directional curvature is dominated by `dirModulus`.

entries.statement: {I : Type u_1} → {O : Type u_2} → [inst : Fintype I] → [inst_1 : Fintype O] → [inst_2 : DecidableEq I] → CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment I O → (I → ℝ) → I → ℝ
entries.source:
/-- Gradient of the normalized envelope `V_env/4`:
`g_k = n^{-1} ∑_{i,j : k∈S_{ij}} {−(∏_{ℓ∈S_{ij}} p_ℓ^{-1}) p_k^{-1}
        + (∏_{ℓ∈S_{ij}} (1−p_ℓ)^{-1}) (1−p_k)^{-1}}`. -/
noncomputable def envelopeGrad (p : I → ℝ) (k : I) : ℝ :=
  (Fintype.card O : ℝ)⁻¹ * ∑ i, ∑ j,
    (if k ∈ E.shared i j then
        -(∏ l ∈ E.shared i j, (p l)⁻¹) * (p k)⁻¹
          + (∏ l ∈ E.shared i j, (1 - p l)⁻¹) * (1 - p k)⁻¹
      else 0)
entries.name: CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment.envelopeGrad

entries.statement: ∀ {I : Type u_1} {O : Type u_2} [inst : Fintype I] [inst_1 : Fintype O] [inst_2 : DecidableEq I] (E : CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment I O) (ε : ℝ), 0 < ε → ContDiff ℝ 2 fun p => E.varEnvelopeExt ε p / 4
entries.source:
/-- The NORMALIZED floored envelope extension `V_env^ext / 4` is `C²`. This is the function whose
Hessian quadratic form the modulus `dirModulus` measures. -/
lemma envelopeQuarterExt_contDiff_two (ε : ℝ) (hε : 0 < ε) :
    ContDiff ℝ 2 (fun p : I → ℝ => E.varEnvelopeExt ε p / 4) := by
  unfold varEnvelopeExt
  fun_prop (disch := assumption)
entries.name: CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment.envelopeQuarterExt_contDiff_two
entries.doc:
The NORMALIZED floored envelope extension `V_env^ext / 4` is `C²`. This is the function whose
Hessian quadratic form the modulus `dirModulus` measures.

entries.statement: ∀ {I : Type u_1} {O : Type u_2} [inst : Fintype I] [inst_1 : Fintype O] [inst_2 : DecidableEq I] (E : CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment I O) (ε B : ℝ), 0 < ε → ∀ {q : I → ℝ}, q ∈ CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.feasibleSet ε B → ∀ {d : I → ℝ}, (∀ (k : I), |d k| ≤ 1) → (fun s => (E.varEnvelope fun k => q k + s * d k) / 4) =ᶠ[nhds 0] fun s => (E.varEnvelopeExt ε fun k => q k + s * d k) / 4
entries.source:
/-- On an `ε/2`-ball of parameters, a line through a feasible point in a direction bounded by `1`
stays in the region where `recipC` has saturated, so `V_env` and `V_env^ext` agree there. -/
lemma envelope_line_eventuallyEq (ε B : ℝ) (hε : 0 < ε) {q : I → ℝ}
    (hq : q ∈ feasibleSet (I := I) ε B) {d : I → ℝ} (hd : ∀ k, |d k| ≤ 1) :
    (fun s : ℝ => E.varEnvelope (fun k => q k + s * d k) / 4)
      =ᶠ[𝓝 (0 : ℝ)] (fun s : ℝ => E.varEnvelopeExt ε (fun k => q k + s * d k) / 4) := by
  filter_upwards [Ioo_mem_nhds (a := -(ε / 2)) (b := ε / 2)
    (by linarith) (by linarith)] with s hs
  have hsabs : |s| < ε / 2 := (abs_lt).mpr ⟨by linarith [hs.1], by linarith [hs.2]⟩
  have hbox : ∀ k, ε / 2 ≤ q k + s * d k ∧ ε / 2 ≤ 1 - (q k + s * d k) := by
    intro k
    have hsd : |s * d k| ≤ ε / 2 := by
      calc
        |s * d k| = |s| * |d k| := abs_mul _ _
        _ ≤ |s| * 1 := mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left (hd k) (abs_nonneg s)
        _ ≤ ε / 2 := le_of_lt (by simpa using hsabs)
    have hqk := hq.floor k
    constructor <;> linarith [le_abs_self (s * d k), neg_le_abs (s * d k)]
  exact congrArg (fun x : ℝ => x / 4)
    (E.varEnvelopeExt_eq_varEnvelope_of_box hε (fun k => (hbox k).1)
      (fun k => (hbox k).2)).symm
entries.name: CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment.envelope_line_eventuallyEq
entries.doc:
On an `ε/2`-ball of parameters, a line through a feasible point in a direction bounded by `1`
stays in the region where `recipC` has saturated, so `V_env` and `V_env^ext` agree there.

entries.statement: {I : Type u_1} → {O : Type u_2} → [inst : Fintype I] → [inst_1 : Fintype O] → [inst_2 : DecidableEq I] → CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment I O → (I → Bool) → O → ℝ
entries.source:
/-- All-control exposure indicator `C_i(Z) = ∏_{k∈N_i} (1−Z_k)`. -/
noncomputable def expC (z : I → Bool) (i : O) : ℝ :=
  ∏ k ∈ E.N i, (if z k then (0 : ℝ) else 1)   -- @realizes C_i(Z)(product over N_i of 1−Z_k)
entries.name: CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment.expC
entries.doc: All-control exposure indicator `C_i(Z) = ∏_{k∈N_i} (1−Z_k)`.

entries.statement: {I : Type u_1} → {O : Type u_2} → [inst : Fintype I] → [inst_1 : Fintype O] → [inst_2 : DecidableEq I] → CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment I O → (I → Bool) → O → ℝ
entries.source:
/-- All-treated exposure indicator `T_i(Z) = ∏_{k∈N_i} Z_k`. -/
noncomputable def expT (z : I → Bool) (i : O) : ℝ :=
  ∏ k ∈ E.N i, (if z k then (1 : ℝ) else 0)   -- @realizes T_i(Z)(product over N_i of Z_k); @realizes Z_k(coordinate assignment z k : Bool realizing {0,1} via if z k then 1 else 0); @realizes Z(assignment vector z : I→Bool = {0,1}^{m_n})
entries.name: CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment.expT
entries.doc: All-treated exposure indicator `T_i(Z) = ∏_{k∈N_i} Z_k`.

entries.statement: ∀ {I : Type u_1} {O : Type u_2} [inst : Fintype I] [inst_1 : Fintype O] [inst_2 : DecidableEq I] (E : CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment I O) (ε B : ℝ), 0 < ε → ∀ {q : I → ℝ}, q ∈ CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.feasibleSet ε B → ∀ (d : I → ℝ), (fderiv ℝ (fun p => E.varEnvelopeExt ε p / 4) q) d = ∑ k, d k * E.envelopeGrad q k
entries.source:
/-- The directional derivative of the normalized floored envelope is the gradient-score
contraction `∑ k, d k * g_k(q)`. -/
lemma fderiv_envelopeQuarterExt_apply (ε B : ℝ) (hε : 0 < ε) {q : I → ℝ}
    (hq : q ∈ feasibleSet (I := I) ε B) (d : I → ℝ) :
    fderiv ℝ (fun p : I → ℝ => E.varEnvelopeExt ε p / 4) q d =
      ∑ k, d k * E.envelopeGrad q k := by
  classical
  calc
    fderiv ℝ (fun p : I → ℝ => E.varEnvelopeExt ε p / 4) q d =
        ∑ k, d k * fderiv ℝ (fun p : I → ℝ => E.varEnvelopeExt ε p / 4) q
          (Pi.single k 1) := by
      conv_lhs => rw [pi_eq_sum_univ d]
      rw [map_sum]
      simp_rw [map_smul]
      simp only [smul_eq_mul]
      apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
      intro x _
      congr 1
      apply congrArg (fderiv ℝ (fun p : I → ℝ => E.varEnvelopeExt ε p / 4) q)
      funext j
      by_cases h : x = j
      · subst j
        simp
      · have h' : j ≠ x := Ne.symm h
        simp [h, h']
    _ = ∑ k, d k * E.envelopeGrad q k := by
      apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
      intro k _
      rw [E.fderiv_envelopeQuarterExt_single ε B hε hq k]
entries.name: CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment.fderiv_envelopeQuarterExt_apply
entries.doc:
The directional derivative of the normalized floored envelope is the gradient-score
contraction `∑ k, d k * g_k(q)`.

entries.statement: ∀ {I : Type u_1} {O : Type u_2} [inst : Fintype I] [inst_1 : Fintype O] [inst_2 : DecidableEq I] (E : CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment I O) (ε B : ℝ), 0 < ε → ∀ {q : I → ℝ}, q ∈ CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.feasibleSet ε B → ∀ (k : I), (fderiv ℝ (fun p => E.varEnvelopeExt ε p / 4) q) (Pi.single k 1) = E.envelopeGrad q k
entries.source:
/-- Partial derivative of the normalized floored envelope in coordinate `k`, in the Pi space.
This is `deriv_varEnvelope_div_four_coord_line` (now public in `TConvexDesign`) restated as an
`fderiv` applied to the basis vector `Pi.single k 1`. -/
lemma fderiv_envelopeQuarterExt_single (ε B : ℝ) (hε : 0 < ε) {q : I → ℝ}
    (hq : q ∈ feasibleSet (I := I) ε B) (k : I) :
    fderiv ℝ (fun p : I → ℝ => E.varEnvelopeExt ε p / 4) q (Pi.single k 1) =
      E.envelopeGrad q k := by
  let f : (I → ℝ) → ℝ := fun p => E.varEnvelopeExt ε p / 4
  let e : I → ℝ := Pi.single k 1
  have he : ∀ l, |e l| ≤ 1 := by
    intro l
    by_cases hl : l = k
    · subst l
      simp [e]
    · simp [e, hl]
  have henv := E.envelope_line_eventuallyEq ε B hε hq he
  have hextline :
      (fun s : ℝ => f (q + s • e)) =
        (fun s : ℝ => E.varEnvelopeExt ε (fun l => q l + s * e l) / 4) := by
    funext s
    apply congrArg (fun p : I → ℝ => E.varEnvelopeExt ε p / 4)
    funext l
    simp [smul_eq_mul]
  have hcoord :
      (fun s : ℝ => E.varEnvelope (fun l => q l + s * e l) / 4) =
        (fun s : ℝ => E.varEnvelope (fun l => q l + (if l = k then s else 0)) / 4) := by
    funext s
    apply congrArg (fun p : I → ℝ => E.varEnvelope p / 4)
    funext l
    by_cases hl : l = k <;> simp [e, hl]
  calc
    fderiv ℝ f q e = deriv (fun s : ℝ => f (q + s • e)) 0 := by
      rw [deriv_line]
      · simp
      · simpa [f] using (E.envelopeQuarterExt_contDiff_two ε hε).differentiable two_ne_zero
    _ = deriv (fun s : ℝ => E.varEnvelopeExt ε (fun l => q l + s * e l) / 4) 0 := by
      rw [hextline]
    _ = deriv (fun s : ℝ => E.varEnvelope (fun l => q l + s * e l) / 4) 0 :=
      henv.deriv_eq.symm
    _ = deriv (fun s : ℝ => E.varEnvelope (fun l => q l + (if l = k then s else 0)) / 4) 0 := by
      rw [hcoord]
    _ = E.envelopeGrad q k :=
      deriv_varEnvelope_div_four_coord_line E ε B hε hq k
entries.name: CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment.fderiv_envelopeQuarterExt_single
entries.doc:
Partial derivative of the normalized floored envelope in coordinate `k`, in the Pi space.
This is `deriv_varEnvelope_div_four_coord_line` (now public in `TConvexDesign`) restated as an
`fderiv` applied to the basis vector `Pi.single k 1`.

entries.statement: {I : Type u_1} → {O : Type u_2} → [inst : Fintype I] → [inst_1 : Fintype O] → [inst_2 : DecidableEq I] → CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment I O → I → ℝ
entries.source:
/-- Observable degree-dispersion weight
`h_k(G_n) = n^{-1} ∑_{i,j : k∈S_{ij}} |S_{ij}|^{-1}`. -/
noncomputable def hWeight (k : I) : ℝ :=
  (Fintype.card O : ℝ)⁻¹ * ∑ i, ∑ j, (if k ∈ E.shared i j then ((E.shared i j).card : ℝ)⁻¹ else 0)
entries.name: CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment.hWeight
entries.doc:
Observable degree-dispersion weight
`h_k(G_n) = n^{-1} ∑_{i,j : k∈S_{ij}} |S_{ij}|^{-1}`.

entries.statement: {I : Type u_1} → {O : Type u_2} → [inst : Fintype I] → [inst_1 : Fintype O] → [inst_2 : DecidableEq I] → CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment I O → (I → ℝ) → (I → Bool) → ℝ × ℝ
entries.source:
/-- Exposure-weighted Hájek denominators `(D_1, D_0)` with
`D_1 = ∑_i T_i/π_i^1` and `D_0 = ∑_i C_i/π_i^0`. -/
noncomputable def hajekDenominators (p : I → ℝ) (z : I → Bool) : ℝ × ℝ :=
  -- @realizes D_1(p,Z)(first component ∑_i T_i(z)/π_i^1(p); space [0,∞): each `expT z i` is a product of
  -- `{0,1}`-valued factors, hence ≥ 0, and `piT p i > 0` on the feasible box ε ≤ p_k ≤ 1−ε)
  -- @realizes D_0(p,Z)(second component ∑_i C_i(z)/π_i^0(p); space [0,∞): each `expC z i` is a product of
  -- `{0,1}`-valued factors, hence ≥ 0, and `piC p i > 0` on the feasible box ε ≤ p_k ≤ 1−ε)
  (∑ i, E.expT z i / E.piT p i, ∑ i, E.expC z i / E.piC p i)
entries.name: CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment.hajekDenominators
entries.doc:
Exposure-weighted Hájek denominators `(D_1, D_0)` with
`D_1 = ∑_i T_i/π_i^1` and `D_0 = ∑_i C_i/π_i^0`.

entries.statement: {I : Type u_1} → {O : Type u_2} → [inst : Fintype I] → [inst_1 : Fintype O] → [inst_2 : DecidableEq I] → CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment I O → (I → ℝ) → (I → Bool) → ℝ
entries.source:
/-- The heterogeneous Bernoulli Hájek estimator with the explicit zero-denominator
convention (each arm is `0` when its denominator vanishes). -/
noncomputable def hajekEstimator (p : I → ℝ) (z : I → Bool) : ℝ :=
  (if 0 < (E.hajekDenominators p z).1 then
      (∑ i, E.expT z i * E.Yfun i z / E.piT p i) / (E.hajekDenominators p z).1 else 0)
  - (if 0 < (E.hajekDenominators p z).2 then
      (∑ i, E.expC z i * E.Yfun i z / E.piC p i) / (E.hajekDenominators p z).2 else 0)
entries.name: CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment.hajekEstimator
entries.doc:
The heterogeneous Bernoulli Hájek estimator with the explicit zero-denominator
convention (each arm is `0` when its denominator vanishes).

entries.statement: {I : Type u_1} → {O : Type u_2} → [inst : Fintype I] → [inst_1 : Fintype O] → [inst_2 : DecidableEq I] → CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment I O → (I → ℝ) → (I → Bool) → O → ℝ
entries.source:
/-- First-order heterogeneous Hájek linearization
`η_i = (T_i/π_i^1 − 1)(Y_i^1 − μ_1) − (C_i/π_i^0 − 1)(Y_i^0 − μ_0)`. -/
noncomputable def linScore (p : I → ℝ) (z : I → Bool) (i : O) : ℝ :=
  (E.expT z i / E.piT p i - 1) * (E.Y1 i - E.mu1)
    - (E.expC z i / E.piC p i - 1) * (E.Y0 i - E.mu0)
entries.name: CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment.linScore
entries.doc:
First-order heterogeneous Hájek linearization
`η_i = (T_i/π_i^1 − 1)(Y_i^1 − μ_1) − (C_i/π_i^0 − 1)(Y_i^0 − μ_0)`.

entries.statement: {I : Type u_1} → {O : Type u_2} → [inst : Fintype I] → [inst_1 : Fintype O] → [inst_2 : DecidableEq I] → CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment I O → ℝ
entries.source:
/-- Finite-population all-control mean `μ_0 = n^{-1} ∑_i Y_i^0`. -/
noncomputable def mu0 : ℝ := (Fintype.card O : ℝ)⁻¹ * ∑ i, E.Y0 i   -- @realizes mu_0(mean of Y_i^0)
entries.name: CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment.mu0
entries.doc: Finite-population all-control mean `μ_0 = n^{-1} ∑_i Y_i^0`.

entries.statement: {I : Type u_1} → {O : Type u_2} → [inst : Fintype I] → [inst_1 : Fintype O] → [inst_2 : DecidableEq I] → CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment I O → ℝ
entries.source:
/-- Finite-population all-treated mean `μ_1 = n^{-1} ∑_i Y_i^1`. -/
noncomputable def mu1 : ℝ := (Fintype.card O : ℝ)⁻¹ * ∑ i, E.Y1 i   -- @realizes mu_1(mean of Y_i^1)
entries.name: CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment.mu1
entries.doc: Finite-population all-treated mean `μ_1 = n^{-1} ∑_i Y_i^1`.

entries.statement: {I : Type u_1} → {O : Type u_2} → [inst : Fintype I] → [inst_1 : Fintype O] → [inst_2 : DecidableEq I] → CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment I O → O → Finset O
entries.name: CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment.overlapNbrs
entries.doc: Overlap-dependency neighbors of `i`: `{j : S_{ij} ≠ ∅}`.

entries.statement: {I : Type u_1} → {O : Type u_2} → [inst : Fintype I] → [inst_1 : Fintype O] → [inst_2 : DecidableEq I] → CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment I O → (I → ℝ) → O → ℝ
entries.source:
/-- All-control exposure probability `π_i^0(p) = ∏_{k∈N_i} (1−p_k)`. -/
noncomputable def piC (p : I → ℝ) (i : O) : ℝ :=
  ∏ k ∈ E.N i, (1 - p k)   -- @realizes pi_i^0(p)(product over N_i of 1−p_k; range (0,1] via p∈[0,1] from ProbVector/PositivityFloor)
entries.name: CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment.piC
entries.doc: All-control exposure probability `π_i^0(p) = ∏_{k∈N_i} (1−p_k)`.

entries.statement: {I : Type u_1} → {O : Type u_2} → [inst : Fintype I] → [inst_1 : Fintype O] → [inst_2 : DecidableEq I] → CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment I O → (I → ℝ) → O → ℝ
entries.source:
/-- All-treated exposure probability `π_i^1(p) = ∏_{k∈N_i} p_k`. -/
noncomputable def piT (p : I → ℝ) (i : O) : ℝ :=
  ∏ k ∈ E.N i, p k   -- @realizes pi_i^1(p)(product over N_i of p_k; range (0,1] via p∈[0,1] from ProbVector/PositivityFloor)
entries.name: CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment.piT
entries.doc: All-treated exposure probability `π_i^1(p) = ∏_{k∈N_i} p_k`.

entries.statement: {I : Type u_1} → {O : Type u_2} → [inst : Fintype I] → [inst_1 : Fintype O] → [inst_2 : DecidableEq I] → CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment I O → (I → ℝ) → O → O → ℝ
entries.source:
/-- Control overlap load `r_{ij}^0(G_n,p) = 1{S_{ij}≠∅}(∏_{k∈S_{ij}} (1−p_k)^{-1} − 1)`. -/
noncomputable def r0 (p : I → ℝ) (i j : O) : ℝ :=
  if 0 < (E.shared i j).card then (∏ k ∈ E.shared i j, (1 - p k)⁻¹) - 1 else 0
entries.name: CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment.r0
entries.doc: Control overlap load `r_{ij}^0(G_n,p) = 1{S_{ij}≠∅}(∏_{k∈S_{ij}} (1−p_k)^{-1} − 1)`.

entries.statement: {I : Type u_1} → {O : Type u_2} → [inst : Fintype I] → [inst_1 : Fintype O] → [inst_2 : DecidableEq I] → CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment I O → ℝ → (I → ℝ) → O → O → ℝ
entries.source:
/-- Extends the control-overlap kernel to all propensity vectors by smoothly regularizing reciprocal control probabilities; it agrees with the original kernel away from one. -/
noncomputable def r0Ext (ε : ℝ) (p : I → ℝ) (i j : O) : ℝ :=
  if 0 < (E.shared i j).card then (∏ k ∈ E.shared i j, recipC ε (1 - p k)) - 1 else 0
entries.name: CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment.r0Ext
entries.doc: Extends the control-overlap kernel to all propensity vectors by smoothly regularizing reciprocal control probabilities; it agrees with the original kernel away from one.

entries.statement: ∀ {I : Type u_1} {O : Type u_2} [inst : Fintype I] [inst_1 : Fintype O] [inst_2 : DecidableEq I] (E : CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment I O) (ε : ℝ), 0 < ε → ∀ (i j : O), ContDiff ℝ 1 fun p => E.r0Ext ε p i j
entries.source:
/-- For a positive cutoff, the regularized control-overlap kernel is continuously differentiable in all propensity coordinates. -/
@[fun_prop]
lemma r0Ext_contDiff (ε : ℝ) (hε : 0 < ε) (i j : O) :
    ContDiff ℝ 1 (fun p : I → ℝ => E.r0Ext ε p i j) := by
  unfold r0Ext
  by_cases hij : 0 < (E.shared i j).card
  · simp [hij]
    fun_prop (disch := assumption)
  · simp [hij]
    fun_prop
entries.name: CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment.r0Ext_contDiff
entries.doc: For a positive cutoff, the regularized control-overlap kernel is continuously differentiable in all propensity coordinates.

entries.statement: ∀ {I : Type u_1} {O : Type u_2} [inst : Fintype I] [inst_1 : Fintype O] [inst_2 : DecidableEq I] (E : CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment I O) (ε : ℝ), 0 < ε → ∀ (i j : O), ContDiff ℝ 2 fun p => E.r0Ext ε p i j
entries.source:
/-- The floored control-load extension is `C²`. -/
@[fun_prop]
lemma r0Ext_contDiff_two (ε : ℝ) (hε : 0 < ε) (i j : O) :
    ContDiff ℝ 2 (fun p : I → ℝ => E.r0Ext ε p i j) := by
  unfold r0Ext
  by_cases hij : 0 < (E.shared i j).card
  · simp only [hij, ↓reduceIte]
    fun_prop (disch := assumption)
  · simp only [hij, ↓reduceIte]
    fun_prop
entries.name: CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment.r0Ext_contDiff_two
entries.doc: The floored control-load extension is `C²`.

entries.statement: ∀ {I : Type u_1} {O : Type u_2} [inst : Fintype I] [inst_1 : Fintype O] [inst_2 : DecidableEq I] (E : CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment I O) {ε : ℝ}, 0 < ε → ∀ {p : I → ℝ}, (∀ (k : I), ε / 2 ≤ 1 - p k) → ∀ (i j : O), E.r0Ext ε p i j = E.r0 p i j
entries.source:
/-- When every control probability is at least half the cutoff, the regularized control-overlap kernel equals the original control kernel. -/
lemma r0Ext_eq_r0_of_box {ε : ℝ} (hε : 0 < ε) {p : I → ℝ}
    (hp : ∀ k, ε / 2 ≤ 1 - p k) (i j : O) :
    E.r0Ext ε p i j = E.r0 p i j := by
  unfold r0Ext BipartiteExperiment.r0
  by_cases hij : 0 < (E.shared i j).card
  · simp [hij]
    calc
      ∏ k ∈ E.shared i j, recipC ε (1 - p k) =
          ∏ k ∈ E.shared i j, (1 - p k)⁻¹ := by
        exact Finset.prod_congr rfl (by intro k _hk; exact recipC_eq_inv hε (hp k))
      _ = (∏ k ∈ E.shared i j, (1 - p k))⁻¹ := by
        rw [Finset.prod_inv_distrib]
  · simp [hij]
entries.name: CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment.r0Ext_eq_r0_of_box
entries.doc: When every control probability is at least half the cutoff, the regularized control-overlap kernel equals the original control kernel.

entries.statement: {I : Type u_1} → {O : Type u_2} → [inst : Fintype I] → [inst_1 : Fintype O] → [inst_2 : DecidableEq I] → CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment I O → (I → ℝ) → O → O → ℝ
entries.source:
/-- Treated overlap load `r_{ij}^1(G_n,p) = 1{S_{ij}≠∅}(∏_{k∈S_{ij}} p_k^{-1} − 1)`. -/
noncomputable def r1 (p : I → ℝ) (i j : O) : ℝ :=
  if 0 < (E.shared i j).card then (∏ k ∈ E.shared i j, (p k)⁻¹) - 1 else 0
entries.name: CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment.r1
entries.doc: Treated overlap load `r_{ij}^1(G_n,p) = 1{S_{ij}≠∅}(∏_{k∈S_{ij}} p_k^{-1} − 1)`.

entries.statement: {I : Type u_1} → {O : Type u_2} → [inst : Fintype I] → [inst_1 : Fintype O] → [inst_2 : DecidableEq I] → CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment I O → O → O → ℝ
entries.name: CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment.r10
entries.doc: Cross-arm overlap load `r_{ij}^{10}(G_n) = 1{S_{ij}≠∅}`.

entries.statement: {I : Type u_1} → {O : Type u_2} → [inst : Fintype I] → [inst_1 : Fintype O] → [inst_2 : DecidableEq I] → CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment I O → ℝ → (I → ℝ) → O → O → ℝ

/-- Extends the treated-overlap kernel to all propensity vectors by replacing reciprocal probabilities with a smooth cutoff reciprocal; it agrees with the original kernel away from zero. -/
noncomputable def r1Ext (ε : ℝ) (p : I → ℝ) (i j : O) : ℝ :=
  if 0 < (E.shared i j).card then (∏ k ∈ E.shared i j, recipC ε (p k)) - 1 else 0

entries.doc: Extends the treated-overlap kernel to all propensity vectors by replacing reciprocal probabilities with a smooth cutoff reciprocal; it agrees with the original kernel away from zero.

entries.source:

@[fun_prop]
lemma r1Ext_contDiff (ε : ℝ) (hε : 0 < ε) (i j : O) :

entries.statement: ∀ {I : Type u_1} {O : Type u_2} [inst : Fintype I] [inst_1 : Fintype O] [inst_2 : DecidableEq I] (E : CausalSmith.Experimentation.BipartiteMinimaxDesign.BipartiteExperiment I O) (ε : ℝ), 0 < ε → ∀ (i j : O), ContDiff ℝ 2 fun p => E.r1Ext ε p i j

/-- The floored treated-load extension is `C²` (it is `C¹` by `r1Ext_contDiff`; the only
input that needed strengthening is `recipC`, now `Cⁿ` for every `n`). -/
@[fun_prop]
lemma r1Ext_contDiff_two (ε : ℝ) (hε : 0 < ε) (i j : O) :
    ContDiff ℝ 2 (fun p : I → ℝ => E.r1Ext ε p i j) := by
  unfold r1Ext
  by_cases hij : 0 < (E.shared i j).card
  · simp only [hij, ↓reduceIte]
    fun_prop (disch := assumption)
  · simp only [hij, ↓reduceIte]
    fun_prop

The floored treated-load extension is `C²` (it is `C¹` by `r1Ext_contDiff`; the only
